MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Nevin, Esq., counsel for Plaintiff Thomas Sinclair; Andrew Sharp, Esq., ccounsel for remaining Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co.

Court and counsel review the facts to support consolidation of the three cases: Sinclair, Talley, and Draper. Mr. Nevin represents that none of the Plaintiffs are well enough to testify at trial and their testimony will be presented by video deposition and deposition transcript reading. Counsel advise the Court as to which experts will be testifying in each case, to what extent they overlap, and the general extent of their anticipated testimony. The Court examines Mr. Sharp as to what prejudice will occasion his client if the cases are consolidated, particularly with respect to the jury's ability to distinguish the injuries and causation as to the three plaintiffs.

After further discussion, the Court will tentatively consolidate the three cases pending the determination of the appropriate standard of causation [e.g. "but for" vs. "substantial factor"] to be determined before trial. Mr. Nevin is to prepare an Order provisionally consolidating 276622, 276623, and 276633. Final determination of consolidation will be determined at hearing on August 19, 2019.

Court and counsel discuss additional matters to be resolved pre-trial in motions in limine, including the scope of page/line designations. Court recesses for counsel to determine the format and timing of the pre-trial motions.

After recess, counsel advise the Court that they will address all pre-trial issues by motions in limine to be filed by August 7, 2019 with opposition due by August 12, 2019. No replies will be filed. Page/line deignations will be filed by August 5, 2019, opposition by August 12, 2019, and reply, if any, by August 14, 2019. Hearing on all motions and page/line designations will be August 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m.
